articles.title: Why We Built DialPhone: The Business Phone Problem Nobody Was Solving
articles.body:
Every VoIP provider in 2026 advertises the same things: unlimited calling, 99.999% uptime, AI-powered everything. They compete on feature count. Nobody competes on the things that actually matter to a 30-person company trying to get work done.

That is why we built [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com).

## The Problem We Kept Seeing

Before DialPhone, our team spent 15 years as VoIP consultants. We deployed phone systems for 400+ businesses. And the same complaints came up at every single deployment:

**"The mobile app doesn't ring half the time."**
Providers treat the mobile app as an afterthought. The desktop client gets all the engineering attention. But in 2026, 60% of business calls are taken on mobile. If the app misses notifications, employees miss calls, and the company loses revenue.

**"Why am I paying extra for call recording?"**
Call recording is a basic business requirement. It should not be a $5-15/user/month add-on. It should be included.

**"I called support and waited 40 minutes."**
The provider has 2 million users and 50 support agents. You do the math.

**"My per-user cost went up 25% at renewal."**
Introductory pricing that doubles after year one. Buried in page 47 of the contract.

## What DialPhone Does Differently

| What Others Do | What DialPhone Does |
|---------------|--------------------|
| Mobile app is secondary | Mobile app is engineered first |
| Recording is an add-on | Recording included in every plan |
| Support queue: 20-40 minutes | Support: < 5 minutes to a human |
| Introductory pricing tricks | Same price month 1 as month 36 |
| 3-year contracts | Month-to-month, cancel anytime |
| 50+ features, 5 work well | 20 features, all work perfectly |

## The Numbers After 2 Years

| Metric | Industry Average | DialPhone |
|--------|-----------------|----------|
| Mobile app notification reliability | 78% | 97% |
| Support response time | 22 minutes | 3 minutes |
| Customer retention (12-month) | 72% | 94% |
| Average MOS score | 3.9 | 4.3 |
| Price increase at renewal | 8-15% | 0% |

## Who DialPhone Is For

We are not for everyone. We are specifically for:
- Companies with 5-200 employees
- Teams that rely on mobile (field workers, remote, hybrid)
- Businesses that value simplicity over feature count
- Organizations that need call recording without paying extra
- Anyone who has been burned by a VoIP provider before

If you need 500+ seats, global PSTN coverage in 40 countries, or AI sentiment analysis — we are not the right fit. RingCentral or 8x8 serve that market well.

But if you want a phone system that works reliably, costs what it says it costs, and has support that actually answers — try [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com). 30-day free trial. No credit card. No sales calls unless you ask for one.

articles.title: The Small Business Phone System Buyer's Guide for 2026
articles.body:
If you are a small business owner looking at phone systems in 2026, the options are overwhelming. Here is the no-nonsense guide from someone who has helped 400+ businesses make this decision.

## Step 1: How Many People Need Phones?

| Team Size | What You Need | Budget |
|-----------|-------------|--------|
| 1-5 | Basic VoIP with mobile app | $19-28/user/month |
| 5-15 | VoIP with auto-attendant + ring groups | $22-30/user/month |
| 15-50 | Full UC with CRM integration | $24-35/user/month |
| 50+ | Enterprise with call center features | $28-45/user/month |

## Step 2: What Features Do You Actually Need?

Every provider will try to sell you 50+ features. Here are the only ones that matter:

**Must have (non-negotiable):**
- Unlimited domestic calling
- Mobile app (iOS + Android)
- Voicemail with email delivery
- Auto-attendant ("Press 1 for sales")
- Call transfer

**Important (for most businesses):**
- Call recording (included, not add-on)
- CRM integration
- Ring groups
- Video conferencing

**Nice to have (not worth paying extra):**
- AI transcription
- Sentiment analysis
- Custom IVR flows
- Hot desking

## Step 3: The 3 Questions That Eliminate Bad Providers

**Q1: "Is call recording included or an add-on?"**
If it is an add-on, the provider is nickel-and-diming you. Recording is table stakes in 2026.

**Q2: "What is your ACTUAL uptime over the past 12 months?"**
Not the SLA target. The measured uptime. If they cannot answer, they do not track it.

**Q3: "Can I cancel month-to-month?"**
If they require annual or multi-year commitments, they lack confidence in keeping you through service quality.

## Step 4: The Trial Test

Get a free trial from your top 2 providers. For 1 week:
1. Make 10 calls per day — rate audio quality
2. Use the mobile app exclusively for 2 days
3. Call their support line at 10 AM and 8 PM — time the wait
4. Have your office manager try adding a user and changing a ring group

The provider that scores higher on these 4 tests is the one you should choose. Not the one with the longest feature list.

## My Recommendation

For small businesses under 200 employees, [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) consistently wins the trial test. $24/user/month with everything included — recording, auto-attendant, mobile app, video, CRM integration. Month-to-month. 30-day free trial.

But do not take my word for it. Run the trial test yourself.
